prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `c920a713`.
codex_response:
Implemented `c920a713` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: each nonzero color is a damaged rectangular border, the unique intact bbox-border color is the center, the remaining colors are recovered outward by progressively explaining bbox-border intrusions, and the output is the corresponding concentric square-ring image.

I used the hint only for the “concentric rings” part and discarded the “simple contact chain” part. The official examples do not form a plain adjacency chain, so I documented the correction in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_c920a713/notes.md). No new reusable primitive was needed. The generator matches the family by constructing one connected damaged frame per color in a nested spiral/shared-side layout, which visually tracks the official broken-frame pattern while preserving the same recovery rule.

Validation passed: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/c920a713/previews/preview_01.png).
